top_signals.title: Japan 10-Year Bond Sale Sees Weaker Demand Than 12-Month Average
top_signals.summary: Japan’s 10-year government bond auction drew weaker demand than the average for the past year on concerns the Bank of Japan isn’t raising rates quickly enough to contain inflation.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Citi's Luet: China's Franchise Has Grown Very Strongly
top_signals.summary: Marc Luet, Head of Japan, Asia North & Australia and Banking at Citi, says the bank has seen the role of the yuan grow and is hopeful of obtaining a securities license in China. He speaks exclusively on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Burnham tax plans spark investor rush to bank capital gains
top_signals.summary: Entrepreneurs and savers are rushing to bring forward the sale of their businesses or take profit on investments in anticipation of Andy Burnham’s rumoured plans to fund his ambitious devolution agenda by equalising capital gains tax with income tax. Tax and financial advisers reported a sharp uptick in client enquiries on the likely Prime Minister’s [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Inside Arabian Falcons: The Dubai football side managed by Jonjo Shelvey
top_signals.summary: Arabian Falcons have just re-signed Jonjo Shelvey as their manager, with the Dubai football team boasting off-pitch figures such as Jason Puncheon. Matt Hardy investigates Hear someone discussing Arabian Falcons and it would be easy to assume they were talking about either the Peregrine or the Saker – two heritage symbols of the Middle East [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
